items.title: Amidat musaf le-Rosh ha-Shanah, or Amidah prayer for the additional service of New Year
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Entitled Qorban Musaf [קורבן מוסף], this Amidah prayer for the additional service (musaf) of New Year (Rosh ha-Shanah) originates from the Jewish community of Kaifeng in Henan, China. The manuscript has distinctive features such as the use of paper formed by multiple layers resembling rice paper and the square format and "Chinese" features of the script. A note on the front cover states that the manuscript is one of those which the Kaifeng Jews brought to Shanghai in 1851. This occurred following attempts by Christian missionaries to make contact with their community. Notes Folio 13a: handwrit

items.title: Decreto di banco feneratitio
items.creator: Francesco III Gonzaga, Duke of Mantua, 1540-1550
items.desc: Charter issued by the cardinal Ercole Gonzaga and the duchess Margherita Paleologa, in their capacity as tutors of the Duke of Mantua, Margherita's two-year-old son Francesco III Gonzaga. They grant to the Jew Isaac de Massaran license to establish a money-lending bank with two other shareholders from the Jewish community of Mantua. The bank may charge 20% annual interest (noted as lower than the 25% charged by the other four banks in Mantua) and will have the same benefits, immunities, privileges, and honors as the other banks in Mantua. Written in Mantua in 14 lines of humanistic cursive scr

items.title: Ekhah rabati ʻim targum Malʼeyʼalam yehudit.
items.creator: Elis & Ruth Douer Endowed Fund
items.desc: This is a manuscript of the Midrash Lamentations with translation into Judeo-Malayalam, created in Cochin (today known as Kochi, a city on the south-west coast of India on the Laccadive Sea) by the small community of Cochini Jews during the later nineteenth century; Judeo-Malayalam was the only known Dravidian Jewish language at the time and it was the dialect of the Cochini Jews. The text is from Midrash Ekhah rabah; see ibid., ed. Buber (Vilna: Romm Brothers, 1899), p. 24b. The manuscript is written on commercial lined paper (f. 2-12) and paper ruled in lead (f. 13-28). The layout of the man

items.title: Ets Hayyim
items.creator: Vital, Hayyim ben Joseph, 1542-1620
items.desc: Large manuscript on thick paper containing the first thirty-six gates (שערים) of Hayyim Vital's Tree of Life, an extensive Kabbalistic work in the Lurianic tradition. The work is preceded by several prefaces by the author himself and by Meir ben Judah Leib Poppers. The main text is accompanied by glosses from several scholars, among whom Jacob ben Hayyim Zemah. The manuscript, which records several recensions of Vital's magnum opus, is incomplete, and only runs up to the first part of gate 36. A second hand completes chapter 2 of that gate on folio 202a and adds the beginning of chapter 3. Var

Text loss at corners and upper center of the manuscript; extant text from Part II, Chapters 29-30, and partially 32. items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: This is a collection of fragments from Torah scrolls and mezuzot which likely spans the European, Sefardic, and Jerusalemite scribal customs and rites. The first two items are fragments from Torah scrolls intentionally cut out due to misspelled words (pesulim) and they are cut in a straight line around the column, likely by a razor blade. Both were ruled to be written in 42 lines; the first has 42 lines of text while the second stops mid-word on the 40th line. items.title: Hebrew Bible
items.creator: Unknown
items.dateText: Details of the origin of this manuscript, both date and geography, are unclear; produced in the fourteenth or fifteenth century
items.desc: Illuminated Hebrew Bible in Sephardi script. According to the catalogue of Bezalel Narkiss, the Bible contains French- and Italian-style decorations and should most likely be dated to the last quarter of the fifteenth century. The Bible contains Masorah Magna and Parva and Tiberian vocalisation and cantillation signs. It contains a reference to the year 1325 (i.e. 6 or 26 Tevet 5086), though the original function of that date is unclear. The fragment appears to have been torn from a codex, with the original gutter at recto left (the current recto was originally the verso). items.desc: This is a letter written to David Berlin, Friday 20 Ṭevet 5529 (30 December 1768) by Benjamin Veitel Ephraim, writing in Berlin; Benjamin informs Berlin that he is waiting on the rediscovery of a "rohre" document (?) from the important F.M. (רפ"מ הרמה), which had been lost and unlocateable in all relevant places; the document contained an earlier ruling from Saul Lowenstamm of Amsterdam (1717-1790). items.title: Letter, 1768, Leipzig to David Berlin.
items.creator: Bonn, Mordekhai ben Jakob ha-levi
items.dateText: Written in Leipzig, Germany at the Leipziger Messe (Leipzig Fair).
items.desc: This is a letter written to David Berlin in 5528 (1767-8; Berlin assumed rabbinical duties circa January 1768) by Mordekhai ben Jakob ha-levi Bonn, a physician from of the Altona-Hamburg-Wandsbek community who was at the Leipziger Messe (Leipzig trade fair), who was contacting Berlin as he was in need of a letter of recognition from the rabbi to present to the Leipzig authorities. Bonn insists that the letter was important imminently (possibly needed by other Jewish vendors at the Leipziger Messe) and notes that he is sending all monies earned to his wife (in Altona). items.title: Manuscript leaf from Antiquitates Judaicae, Book 13
items.creator: Josephus, Flavius
items.desc: Leaf from a large manuscript of the Latin translation of the Antiquitates Judaicae of Flavius Josephus. Written in transitional script (or protogothic script) in Italy in the first half of the 12th century. Ruled in lead for 2 columns of at least 50 lines, the leaf is damaged at both the top and the left edges, with roughly half of the inner column missing. Water and worms have both damaged the leaf. 1 2-line initial in red on verso. The leaf was later used as a binding fragment. It is from Book 13 (identified by Peter Toth, British Library), containing text describing the conquest of Ptolemai

items.title: Meirat Enayim
items.creator: Isaac ben Samuel, of Acre
items.desc: Meirat Enayim by Isaac ben Samuel of Acre. Kabbalistic supercommentary on Nahmanides' Commentary on the Torah copied by a certain Rabbi David in Revere in the Po Valley near Mantua. The title of the work most likely refers to the last words of Psalm 19.9 ("enlightening the eyes"): "The precepts of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes." items.title: Mrs. Daw's travel diaries, Volume 1
items.creator: Daw, Mrs.
items.desc: This two-volume diary traces the nine-month journey (April to December 1838) of Mr. and Mrs. W. Daw as they travel from London, England to Moscow, Russia, and their return trip home. Mrs. Daw writes almost daily about their experiences and observations, which are embellished with eighteen watercolor sketches. Mrs. Daw reports on other travelers, captains of vessels, and their travel companion, Mr. White. The couple travels by ship, steamboat, coach, horseback, and train, stopping at small villages and large cities. On the outbound trip from England to Russia, Mr. and Mrs. Daw travel chiefly by

items.title: Nimmuqim al Sefer ha-Shorashim
items.creator: Eliyyahu ben Asher ha-Levi Ashkenazi, 1468?-1549
items.dateText: Possibly produced in the nineteenth century
items.desc: Elia Levita's Arguments (נימוקים) in relation to David Kimhi's dictionary of the Hebrew language known as the Book of Roots (ספר השרשים). The content is in alphabetical order with the lemmata underlined, in two columns. The gold tooling on the spine identifies the manuscript as Nofet Zuf by Abraham Broda: clearly a mistake since the manuscript includes a title page corresponding to the actual contents. Notes Paper fragment reading: in ink "No. 17 Autograph. Unbekannt. / Abraham Broda. Nofet Zuf." and in pencil "52119 / 37 / [...]".

items.title: Piyuṭim ketav yad
items.creator: Amar, D. A
items.lang: In Hebrew, with some passages in Judeo-Arabic (f. 4v, 178r-178v)
items.desc: This is a manuscript of collected Hebrew poetry, or piyutim, from the Moroccan tradition of piyut. Many of the poems are written by David ʻAmar (a 19th century Moroccan paytan), as evidenced by his name appearing in acrostic forms (such as the poem ʻIdan duḥḳah, which has a bold title of Daṿid [ʻAmar] ḥazaḳ, f. 95v-96r); likely, the manuscript was written over a period of time. It appears as if the manuscript was meant as a source of Moroccan and even Casablanca piyut; other authors mentioned include Masʻud (or Massoud) ḥazaḳ, who wrote Piyuṭ ha-RaSh.b.I. (after Shimon bar Yoḥai, f. 9

items.title: Salutations and obeisances from correspondence to David Berlin.
items.creator: Unknown
items.dateText: Written in Altona, then Denmark (today part of Hamburg, Germany).
items.desc: This is a fragment of a portion of a letter written to David Berlin (cut off beyond the lines of salutation prose); this item may have been separated by Berlin so as to prepare the letter text for publication or dissemination (Berlin likely wished to edit these lines from being released as they consist of prose). The writing and the use of a professional scribe may suggest that the letter was originally of correspondence from community dignitaries (such as the parnassim). Notes Title supplied by cataloger. Ms. fragment.

items.title: Sefer Goralot
items.creator: Unknown
items.dateText: Produced in the seventeenth or eighteenth century
items.desc: Composite codex containing two copies of the text Sefer Goralot (Book of Lots). A lot (גורל) is cast to find an answer to a question that is important to the individual casting it. The Book of Lots consists of an introductory text, which includes a prayer to be recited before a lot is cast, and several tables. The last table surveys sixty prophets from Adam to Ezra who give twelve answers to different questions. This table can presumably be consulted in conjunction with the first two, which are subdivided into twelve zodiacal signs plus each sign into five further "faces" of in total sixty pro

items.title: Sefer ha-Mivhar, or Commentary on the Pentateuch
items.creator: Aaron ben Joseph, ha-Rofe, -1320
items.desc: A manuscript copy of Aaron ben Joseph the Doctor's thirteenth-century commentary on the Pentateuch, known as "The Choice Book" (Sefer ha-Mivhar) or "Commentary on the Pentateuch". The title used in the manuscript is Sefer ha-Mivhar ve-Tuv ha-Mishar. Referred to as Aaron the Elder in some manuscripts, the author was deeply influenced by Maimonides' Guide for the Perplexed. He is believed to be the first Karaite to have injected Maimonidean ideas into Karaite mainstream thought. Notes Some marginal notes in different hands. Watermark Circle with bull inside and a star next to the circle (f.iii).

items.title: Segulot ṿe-refuʼot
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: This is a manuscript of segulot (supernatural remedies for the prevention against harm) and other remedies for sicknesses and maladies; the manuscript is anonymous and appears North African in script and in its use of amulet designs. The beginning folios appear to be simple cures, and end with letter amulets (f. 1r-3r); the following portion explains themes of supernatural protection (f. 4r-6v) and ends with an amulet design (f. 6v); the final folios are in Hebrew and Judeo-Arabic, and they contain more material on supernatural remedies. In a modern brown binding with marbled endpapers. Three

items.lang: Hebrew, with two words in Judeo-Arabic (f. 78r)
items.desc: This is a manuscript of the Shulḥan 'Arukh written in North Africa likely during the eighteenth century. It is incomplete; it begins on siman (chapter) 55 se'if (paragraph) 8, and completes the Oraḥ Ḥayyim portion, after which the Ḥoshen Mishpaṭ portion begins, complete until siman 400 se'if 3. For Oraḥ Ḥayyim, the center of the double-column is headed with the current topic of the set of simanim (e.g. Hilkhot xxx...) with a notation on the upper margin about which day of the thirty-day cycle of the Shulḥan 'Arukh completion is below; the move to the next day is marked in the outer mar

Blank leaves inserted to mark lacunae. A number of folios have been more or less mutilated. Folios 288r-290v have been added later, contain the Hebrew text in Arabic transcription of Judges 20:18-35, 21:5-14. The Hebrew vowel-points and accents are given in red

items.title: A daily prayer book with seliḥot (i.e. propitiatory prayers), Ashkenazi rite.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A daily prayer book with seliḥot (i.e. propitiatory prayers), Ashkenazi rite. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Margins too closely cut. Folios 1-12 and 109-118: insertions of probably the 14th century. Folio 185: probably of the 11th or 12th century, Sefardi origin. Folios 179-180: a palimpsest, the underlying text being that of a Latin manuscript of the 10th century, apparently containing ecclesiastical canons. Mosheh ha-Leṿi: inscribed in a half-erased entry, folio 1r. Uri bar Ya῾aḳov ha-Kohen: inscribed at the bottom of folio 1v. Menaḥem ben Uri and Menaḥem ben Menaḥem ha-Ko

items.title: Charter

items.desc: Latin deed with Hebrew acknowledgment Notes Cut along an old fold.

items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A collection of abstracts of Al-Qirqisānī's Kitāb al-anwār. Notes Worming (leaves inlaid before rebinding).

items.title: A fragment of anonymous Karaite legal decisions, in greater part treating on rewards and punishments.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of anonymous Karaite legal decisions, in greater part treating on rewards and punishments. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Slightly worm-eaten.

items.title: A halakhic commentary.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A halakhic commentary. Notes Damaged at the beginning. Much worm-eaten, without loss of text. Fra. Luigi da Bologna: inscribed with his name, 1598 (folio 158r); see 104, and appendix 112-117. Giovanni Domenico Vistorini: inscribed with his name, 1609 (folio 158r); see 85-87.

items.title: Commentary on Leviticus.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An anonymous commentary on the book of Leviticus (fragments). Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Many folios worm-eaten and/or damaged. Stained. Worming with some loss of text.

items.title: Ethical essay in praise of Torah study
items.creator: Unknown

items.title: Guide to the preparation of elixirs
items.creator: Unknown

items.title: Hagadah shel Pesaḥ (הגדה של פסח)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Hagadah for Passover (the 'Golden Hagadah'). Notes Folios 3v-16r are 17th century additions. Folio 2r is a roughly illuminated title-page from the 17th century. Fra Luigi da Bologna: censor's signature, Bologna, February 1599 (101v); 104 and appendix § 112-117. Rabi Yo᾿av Gallicoof Asti, duchy of Savoy: gave the manuscript as a wedding present to his 'learned son-in-law Eliyahu', son of Menaḥem Rava in 1602: inscribed on his added title page (folio 2r). The armorial device of the Gallico and Rava families, added at Carpi in 1602 (folio 16r ). Camillo Jaghel: censor's signature, 1613 (folio 101

items.title: Karaite commentaries
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Two fragments of Arabic commentaries on the book of Psalms. Notes Modern restoration, all the folios stuck on modern paper. A long gap after folio 64. Worm-eaten. Lacuna after folio 111. Margins slightly worm-eaten.

items.title: Karaite commentary on Ecclesiastes.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An Arabic commentary on the book of Ecclesiastes. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Many folios damaged in the margins (modern restoration, stuck on paper). Slightly worm-eaten. Stained.

items.title: Ketuvim (כתובים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Hagiographa, provided with vowel-points and accents. Notes Folio 1r contains a list of Hebrew works, probably the property of a formwer owner of the manuscript. Folio 258v contains a list of Sabbath lessons mainly from the Bible. Lower down there are some kabbalistic recipes. Folio 259r - a record of birth of a daughter, dated 15 July 222 (1462). Moshe Shevaḥ ben Yaʿaḳov, Yaʿaḳov ben Eliyah, Shabtai ben Yaʿaḳov, previous owners, inscribed with their names on folio 258r.

items.title: Ketuvim (כתובים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A portion of the Hagiographa with masorah magna and parva. Notes Incomplete at the beginning and at the end. Numerous lacunae.

items.title: Latin grant with Hebrew quitclaim
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Latin grant with Hebrew quitclaim Notes Smaller slip with Hebrew has been repaired. Centre of main piece splitting along folds and weak from mould damage.

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book with services for Rosh ha-Shanah, Yom Kipur and Sukot, according to the German rite. Notes Defective at the end. Stained. Folio 252 partly mutilated. Folio 1 (a late paper flyleaf) contains an index of seliḥot for Yom Kipur. Yitsḥaḳ ben Eli῾ezer ha-Kohen: inscribed (folio 309v). Matatyah ben Yuda: inscribed (folio 309v); his name (Mataty' b"r Yudah Levi z"l) tooled on the contemporary 14th century binding.

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book with services for Rosh ha-Shanah and Yom Kipur, according to the German rite. Notes Defective at the beginning and at the end. Several lacunae. Stained.

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book, according to the (old) French rite. Notes Defective at the beginning and at the end. On guards.

items.title: Maḥzor Yom Kipur (מחזור יום כיפור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book for Yom Kipur, according to the rite of North Africa. Notes Defective at the beginning. Folios 1, 7r, 45v, 251v-252v, 261r-268r are later. Several leaves damaged and damp, with loss of text. Last leaf mutilated. Folios 2r-6v: a piece by Moses ibn Ezra (the beginning is missing). Folio 1r contains a Latin description of the manuscript. Folio 1v contains an index of poetical pieces between folios 8r and 254v, in later Sefardi cursive hand.

items.title: Midrash Aba Orion (מדרש אבא אריון)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Midrash Aba Orion (aka Midrash Aba Gurion) on the book of Esther. Notes Folio 2r & 2v is a modern paper. Contains a description of the manuscript in Latin. Avraham ben Aharon: inscribed, folio 1v.

items.title: Nevi'im (נביאים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Former and Latter Prophets punctuated and accentuated, with masorah magna and parva, Targum Jonathan and Rashi's commentary. Notes Purchase note, with names deleted, 5119 [1359] (2r); copy of the same purchase note, 17th-18th century. Partially deleted inscription: [.. ..] Bar Elhanan blessed be his memory [.. ..] Mosheh Leṿi (2r). Illegible Latin inscriptions (3r). Entries of births and deaths (268r). Censored by Giovanni Domenico Vistorini: inscribed 'Gio. Dom. Vistorini 1609' (2r, 267v), see 85-87.

items.title: Nevi'im u-Khetuvim (נביאים וכתובים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Former and Latter Prophets and Hagiographa, punctuated and accentuated, with masorah magna and parva.

items.title: Nevi᾿im (נביאים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The book of Kings and the Latter Prophets, accentuated and punctuated, with scanty masorah parva.

items.title: Nevi᾿im u-Khetuvim (נביאים וכתובים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Parts of the Prophets and the Hagiographa, punctuated and accentuated, with masorah magna and parva. Notes There is a lacuna after folio 242. Manuscript is also defective at the end and folio 155 is only partially preserved. Ya῾aḳov ben Yo᾿ets; his inscription within the initial word of the Song of Songs, folio 86v. Dated inscription and memorandum on paper fly-leaf, 1r, 4894 (1136, antedated).

items.title: Perush (פרוש)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Daṿid ben Bo῾az on the book of Exodus (fragments). Notes Imperfect at the end. One leaf missing after folio 100. Several folios stained. Shemu᾿el ben ... ha-Nagid: inscribed, folio 2r.

items.title: Perush Be-midbar (פרוש במדבר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An Arabic commentary on Numbers. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Lacuna after folio 95. Stained.

items.title: Perush Shemot (פרוש שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An Arabic commentary on the weekly sections Be-shalaḥ, Yitro and Mishpaṭim (Exodus 13:17-24:18). Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Lacunae after folios 3 and 6, and quires 17-20 are entirely lost. Stained. Folios 270r-270v - contains doodles, magical squares, etc. Yosef ben Mosheh: inscribed, folio 1v. Mosheh Firūz Ḳara᾿i: inscribed, folio 1r.

items.title: Perush ῾al hafṭarot (פרוש על הפטרות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A Judeo-Arabic commentary on the Hafṭarot of Numbers. Notes Imperfect at the end. A number of pages partially damaged (restored on modern paper); large signs of worming. There are lacunae after folio 1 and 2.

items.title: Perushim
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Fragments of Arabic commentaries on Leviticus. Notes Stained. A number of pages worm-eaten and/or damaged.

items.title: Perushim
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Fragments of Arabic commentaries on Numbers, Leviticus and Deuteronomy. Notes Mounted on guards (modern restoration). Gaps after folio 31. All the leaves are more or less worm-eaten.

items.title: Perushim
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Fragments of Arabic commentaries on Exodus, Numbers and forbidden degrees of marriage. Notes Slightly damaged. The upper part of the leaves is imperfectly preserved. Stuck on modern paper.

items.title: Perushim (פרושים)

items.title: Astronomical anthology.
items.creator: Jacob ben David ben Yom Tov
items.desc: Collection of astronomical texts, including a copy of a treatise on the calendar originally compiled for Pedro IV, King of Aragon, with an almanac of oppositions and conjunctions of the sun and moon and predictions of lunar and solar eclipses; four short works by the 12th-century scientist Abraham Ibn Ezra on an introduction to astrology, choosing the most auspicious moment for a given activity, the zodiac, and astrology concerning humankind collectively; and numerous tables (including tables for converting between Christian and Jewish calendars), diagrams, and illustrations. Notes Ms. codex.

items.title: Karaite codex
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Karaite codex consisting of two incomplete parts written in several hands. The first part (1b-22b) is a Judeo-Arabic text on Esther, perhaps a retelling of the story of Esther. It is incomplete at its end. The second part (23a-82b) comprises Muqaddimat on a number of weekly Torah readings from Genesis, sometimes more than once on the same parashah. The Muqaddimat are transmitted anonymously. It appears none were written by the fifteenth-century Karaite scholar from Cairo Samuel ben Moses al-Maghribi. items.desc: The Tosefta to Seder Mo῾ed, preceded by that to Masekhet Ḥulin. Notes Stained. Folio [74] (a flyleaf) contains a part of Tefilin shel rosh. Simḥah da Lonigo: inscribed, folio 2r.

items.title: Nur al-tsulm by Nathanael ben Isaiah (נור אלצלם)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Nur al-tsulm, an Arabic-Hebrew midrashic commentary on the Pentateuch by Nathanael ben Isaiah. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Lacunae after folio 1, 50, 62, 64, 71.

items.title: Perush
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Three fragments of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Yefet ben 'Eli on Genesis. Notes All folios partially water-damaged. Water-damaged. Water-damaged.

items.title: Perush
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Two fragments of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali on Exodus. Notes All folios partially water-damaged. A large lacuna after folio 65 (lost quires nos. 8-11). Water-damaged. Folio 1r contains the following advice on lending books: לא תעירן כתאבא" ואגעל אלעדר גואבא ואן כאן ולא בד כד אלרהן צואבא.

items.title: Perush (פרוש)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The book of Joshua with a Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi. Notes The first quire has not been preserved. A number of folios have been more or less mutilated. Folio 142 has been added later into the 15th quire.

items.title: Perush Be-midbar (פרוש במדבר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on Numbers (1, 7-8, 15, 20, 24, 31-32, 35-36). Notes Lacunae after folios 7, 15, 16, 21, 30, 38, 48, 50, 52 and 60. Stained.

items.title: Perush Iyov (פרוש איוב)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Job, chapters 1-22. Notes Lacunae after folios: 1, 2, 3, 9, 19, 20, 23, 27, 28, 32, 33, 36, 37, 45, 49, 50, 51, 57, 59, 72, 73, 75, 79, 81, 89, 98, 108. Many folios stained or water-damaged. Shemu᾿el ben Eliyahu Firuz: inscribed in a note, folio 41v.

items.title: Perush Iyov (פרוש איוב)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Job, chapters 23-42. Notes Lacunae after folios: 7, 8, 16, 22, 53, 71, 91, 99, 107, 115, 119, 133, 143. Many folios stained or water-damaged. Folio 150v contains a geneology of the previous owner and a rhyme. Avraham ben Shelomoh ben Shemu᾿el ben Eliyahu ben Aharon ben Yitsḥaḳ ben Eliyahu Firuz: inscribed in a note, folio 1r . An entry recording the name and genealogy of the same owner is also found on folio 150v.

items.title: Perush Mishle (פרוש משלי)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Proverbs. Notes Lacunae after folios: 8, 9, 12, 16, 17, 25, 33 65, 78, 119. Many folios stained or water-damaged.

items.title: Perush Mishle (פרוש משלי)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Proverbs. Notes Lacunae after folios: 5, 13, 27, 58, 78, 93, 101, 111, 149, 157, 172, 173. Imperfect at the end. Many folios stained or water-damaged.

items.title: Perush Shemot
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Two fragments of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Yefet ben 'Eli on Exodus. Notes All folios partially water-damaged. The last quire is imperfect, with a lacuna after folio 140 and 148. Water-damaged. The 15th quire is now imperfect, with a lacuna after folio 194 and 202. Folios 208r-209v: two appended folios, mutilated, written in two different Sefardi hands, of apparently 11 to 12th century, containing fragments of Talmud (Mo῾ed Katan and Eruvin).

items.title: Perush Shemot (פרוש שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali on Exodus 18-20. Notes There are lacunae after folios 2, 34, 44, 103 and 117. Defective at the end. Several folios have been water-damaged and stained. Yeshu῾ah Rofe᾿ ben Mordekhai Rofe᾿: inscribed, folio 1r.

items.title: Perush Shemot (פרוש שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali on Exodus 17:5-20:14. Notes The last quire is imperfect, the last three leaves having been lost. Several folios have been damaged and later restored on modern paper.

items.title: Perush Shir ha-shirim (פרוש שיר השירים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the Song of Songs (fragments). Notes Partially damaged. On folio 119v there is a beginning of a question and answer on a grammatical matter in the Song of Songs.

items.title: Perush mel᾿akhah ḳetanah (פרוש מלאכה קטנה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Medical treatises. Notes Defective at the end. Tidemarks at head and foredge; area of soft/weak paper at foredge. Iron gall ink corrosion. Note in Sefardi cursive script on folio 1v.

items.title: Perush shemot (פרוש שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali on Exodus 34, 35-40. Notes The first and the last quire is imperfect. Stained. Several pages damaged.

items.title: Perush Ṿa-yiḳra (פרוש ויקרא)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on Leviticus (21-24). Notes Lacuna after folios: 18. Aharon ben Nezer ben Ezra᾿ ben Mosheh: inscribed, folio 179r. Melekh Al-avad Al-aziz ibn Firūz: inscribed, folio 178v-179r.

items.title: Perush ῾al hafṭarot (פרוש על הפטרות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A Judeo-Arabic commentary on the Hafṭarot of Exodus. Notes Imperfect at the beginning. A number of pages partially damaged; large signs of book-worming.

items.title: Perushim
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Five fragments of Japhet ben Ali's Judeo-Arabic commentaries on various biblical books. Notes Imperfect. Several folios mutilated. Several leaves damaged. Most folios severely damaged. Stained. Fading ink. Stained. Fading ink.

items.title: Petaḥ devarai (פתח דברי)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Grammatical writings. Notes Stained. Incomplete at the beginning. Yosef Tsaror: inscribed with his name (folio 104v). Shemu᾿el Dafno: inscribed with his name (folio 125v). Don Meir ben Ban: inscribed with his name (folio 125v). Don Avraham ben Ban: inscribed with his name (folio 125v).

items.title: Philosophical writings.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Philosophical writings. Notes Tidemark at top corner. Paper foxing, especially at corners and edges. A few usage stains.

items.title: Piyuṭim (פיוטים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Portions of a collection of piyutim belonging to the Sefardi (North African) rite. Notes Lower margin of the last 20 folios damaged. Stained. A number of notes in a different hand than the bulk of the manuscript, e.g. folios 2r, 3r, 8r, 13r, 37r, etc.).

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Surface dirt ingrained in leather. Some mould and/or salt accretions. Edges slightly horny in places. Damp staining at edges. Slight usage stains.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes A few usage stains, and some spills from long top edge. Patches of surface abrasion.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Dry mould and/or salt accretions. Edge damage and tears. Sewing split in a number of places. Cracking/delamination at folds. Worming and insect damage. Bird excreta. Possible rodent urine contamination. Slight water damage.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Salt or mould efflorescence. Edge damage, surface abrasion, tears, and losses. Text partly illegible. Some old patches with red rot. Dark, hard or stretchy edges from manufacture. Ink often flaked away. Pleats and folds occasionally obscure text. Some parts very wormed at edges and joins.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Lots of old repairs, sewn or glued patches recto or verso. Very damaged edges. Very dirty, with mould/salt accretions. Some pleats and creases. Stained. Worming in part.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Surface accretions, salt, mould, leather bloom, including liquid trickle patterns. Edge damage and tears, including possible red rot to repair patches. Paper fragments adhered to verso. Some pleats obscure text. Various stains, including damp, glue, candle wax. Surface abraded in part. Deliberate delamination and removal of surface. Many splits in sewing. Ink with white crust in places, partly flaked away.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Staining and water damage throughout, in patches. Dirt, mud, accretions, deliberate erasures. Some dark, contracted edges. Worming. Folds. Some abraded surfaces and delamination. Ink falking in parts, with losses of text. Ink offset on verso.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Staining and water damage throughout, in patches. Dirt, mud, accretions, deliberate erasures. Some dark, contracted edges. Worming. Folds. Some abraded surfaces and delamination. Ink falking in parts, with losses of text. Ink offset on verso.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Staining and water damage throughout, in patches. Dirt, mud, accretions, deliberate erasures. Some dark, contracted edges. Worming. Folds. Some abraded surfaces and delamination. Ink falking in parts, with losses of text. Ink offset on verso.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Staining and water damage throughout, in patches. Dirt, mud, accretions, deliberate erasures. Some dark, contracted edges. Worming. Folds. Some abraded surfaces and delamination. Ink falking in parts, with losses of text. Ink offset on verso.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Worming in several phases (repairs wormed in different pattern), grazed surface. Surface delamination, deliberate erasures. Weak edges with tears and losses, a few parts folded under. Creases from rolling. Inks slightly flaking. Some ink spills. Pleat covering text. Soil accretions. Occasional contracted areas at edge with dark, brittle, flaking surface. Usage and damp stains.

items.title: Sefer Torah (ספר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Surface occasionally dry, dark and crazed. A few delaminations. Old repairs, including linen supports on verso, with glue stains. Edge damage. Water marks in part. Stained, with some degraded leather tearing. Ink offset to verso from damp, flaking in places.

items.title: Sefer ha-mivḥar (ספר המבחר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A commentary on the Pentateuch. Notes Partially water-damaged. Stained.

items.title: Sefer ha-zohar (ספר הזוהר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Sefer ha-zohar on the book of Genesis. Notes Eliyahu Anav: inscribed with his name, undated (folio 1r). Laurentius Franguellus (fl. until 1596): inscribed with his name, 1575 (folio 182v); see 78, appendix § 105-110. Hippolite of Ferrara, Franciscan (active 1590-1609, in Cremona?): inscribed with his name, 1601 (folio 182v), see 18-19, 93. Giovanni Domenico Vistorini: inscribed 'Gio. Dom. Vistorini 1609' (folio 182v), see 85-87.

items.title: Shemot (שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.lang: Hebrew; Jewish Babylonian Aramaic (ca. 200-1200 CE); Judeo-Arabic
items.desc: The book of Exodus with a verse-by-verse translation into Aramaic, an Arabic translation of Sa'adia Gaon, and provided with the simple superlinear punctuation. Notes Much stained or blurred in parts, occasionally also a little damaged. Imperfect at the beginning and also having lacunae between some leaves in the body of the mss.

items.title: Sidur (סדור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A daily prayer book. Notes Folios 251v, 252r&v contain attempts by someone learning to write Hebrew.

items.title: Sidur (סדור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A daily prayer book. Notes Lacuna after folio 154. A note of ownership of the Supino family (Mishpaḥat bet ha-ne῾arim Supino): folio 1v.

items.title: Sidur (סדור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A daily prayer book. Notes First 30 folios are much damaged, with partly illegible text. Defective at the beginning, and some leaves are also missing after folio 48. Stained.

items.title: Sidur minhag Sefarad (סדור מנהג ספרד)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A prayer book with services according to the Spanish rite. Notes Usage stains. Folios 197-199 have slight rodent damage.

items.title: Tana"kh (תנ"ך)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Bible, with vowel-points and accents, accompanied with masorah parva. Notes Usage stains. Dirt entries at places. Folio 1r: a short list of books in late cursive hand.

items.title: Tefilot ῾al derekh ha-ḳabalah (תפלות על דרך הקבלה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Kabbalistic prayers. Notes Edges very dark, a few tears at outer end. Severe creasing at inner end. Liquid (glue) and ink stains on verso. Ink with some losses to abrasion or flaking, first few lines illegible.

items.title: Tehilim (תהילים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Psalms, not punctuated. Notes Avraham: inscribed, folio 1r. A former owner's name on the inside of the front cover is not legible, but his father's name was Yitsḥaḳ of Cologne. Written in Italian cursive script.

items.title: The Book of Ecclesiastes.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Ecclesiastes. Notes Darkening at head. Small patches of salt accretion. A few usage stains.

items.title: The Book of Esther.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Esther. Notes Creases. A few usage stains. Dirt along edges and slight tidemark near outer end.

items.title: The Book of Esther.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Esther. Notes Leather often fibrous on verso. First column of text darker and difficult to read. Strap broken off short. Cockling/creasing and vertical marks from rolling. Surface occasionally disturbed by erasures. Slight worming at edges. A few tears, losses, and edges rolling over. Crease between text columns.

items.title: The Book of Lamentations.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Lamentations. Notes Surface disturbed by erasure. Darkening edges and a few text areas. Ink offset to verso from rolling (possible damp).

items.title: The Book of Ruth.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Ruth. Notes A few minor stains, and darkening in spine area.

items.title: The Pentateuch scroll.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Roller handle broken but attached to scroll. Very occasional stains, wax, etc. Occasional and slight mould mottling on verso. Ink flaky in places.

items.title: The Pentateuch scroll.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch scroll. Notes Staining and water damage throughout, in patches. Dirt, mud, accretions, deliberate erasures. Some dark, contracted edges. Worming. Folds. Some abraded surfaces and delamination. Ink falking in parts, with losses of text. Ink offset on verso.

items.title: The Pentateuch, with vowel-points and accents, accompanied by masorah magna and parva, in two volumes.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch, with vowel-points and accents, accompanied by masorah magna and parva, in two volumes. Notes Paper fixed in numerous places with silk gauze. Missing, damaged parts are supplied by a later hand. His colophon is on folio 151v: נשלם תקון זה התאג' .. שנת ב' אלפים ומאה וארבעין שנין לשטרי במאתא כיואן .. והמתקן חתכי [=סאלם בא"ת ב"ש] בן כבוד אבא מנחם בן סעדיה.

items.title: The Song of Songs.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Song of Songs. Notes Damp stains at edges. Salt accretions. Ink offset on verso from damp.

items.title: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Jeremiah.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on the book of Jeremiah. Notes Several of the extant folios have been water-damaged. Lacunae after folios: 1, 2, 8, 9, 18, 23, 35, 38, 46, 57, 69, 75, 83, 103, 111, 117, 149, 151, 153, 204, 212. Eliyahu ben Shelomoh ben Shemu᾿el ben Eliyahu ben Aharon ben Yitsḥaḳ ben Eliyahu Firuz: inscribed, folio 3r.

items.title: Torah (תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentatuech, provided with vowel-points and accents. Notes Ya'akov Kats: inscribed with his name, folio 401r, with a date of 421 (1661).

items.title: Torah ῾im perush Rashi (תורה עם פרוש רש"י)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Pentateuch, accompanied with punctuation and accents, Rashi's commentary and Targum. Notes Ink partly fading. Leṿi Ḥalpan: inscribed, folio 224r. Ben Tsiyon ben Refa'el Mitantso and Daṿid Merts: inscribed with their names in the contract of sale, with a date of 15 Siṿan 283 (08 June 1523), folio 225r. The same folio contains a record of birth of 4 children of the latter owner, dated from 1523 to 1533. Domenico Irosolimitano, Italian censor (active 1585 - 1624): inscribed with his name, 1593 (folio 224v); see 61. Giovanni Domenico Carretto, Italian censor (active 1600 - 1624): inscribed with hi

items.title: Tosafot ῾al masekhet Makot
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Tosafot yeshanim, a collection of talmudic glosses. Notes Partly water-stained. Domenico Carretto: Italian censor (active in the first quarter of the 17th century): inscribed with his name, 1623; see 100-101; folio 153v. Censored by Domenico Irosolimitano, Italian censor (active in the last quarter of the 16th and first quarter of the 17th century): inscribed with his name (folio 153v); see 61. Alessandro Scipione: Italian censor (a convert Jew, active in the last quarter of the 16th and first quarter of the 17th century): inscribed with his name (folio 153v).

items.title: Tsedat ha-derakhim (צדת הדרכים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A medical treatise. Notes Lacunae after folios 14 and 30. Water and mould damage. First page is hardly legible. Several leaves have been amended.

items.title: Two kabbalistic works bound in one volume.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Two kabbalistic works bound in one volume. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Lacuna between folios 87 and 88. Stained. Imperfect at the end. Stained.

items.title: כתובים (Ketuvim)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Parts of the Hagiographa: Psalms, Proverbs, Job, and Five Scrolls. Notes Tidemark at head, sometimes at edges. Front paper leaves repaired/infilled before rebinding. Incomplete at the end.

items.title: פירוש סדר נזיקין (Perush Seder neziḳin)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The commentary of Hananel ben Ḥushiʼel on parts of Seder neziḳin. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Shelomoh ben Avraham Norzi: inscribed, folio 1r. Re'uven ben David mi-Perushah [?]: inscribed, folio 3r. Censored by Giovanni Domenico Carretto, Italian censor (active in the first quarter of the 17th century): inscribed with his name, 1618 (folio 175r ); see 100-101. Censored by Dominico Irosolomitano, Italian censor (active in the last quarter of the 16th and first quarter of the 17th century): inscribed with his name (folio 175v); see 61. Censored by Alessandro Scipione, Itali

items.title: Ḥidushim (חדושים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Contains Novellae on the tractate Ḳidushin. Notes Some of the quires are incomplete. Partly worm-eaten. Stained. Folios 2r and 98v contain doodles. Folios 1r-1v belong to a different manuscript (vellum). Yehudah Ha-Kohen Ashkenazi: inscribed, folio 1r, 3r. Shemu᾿el Casani (?): inscribed in a note of sale, folio 1r.

items.title: Gan Elohim (גן אלהים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Gan Elohim, a supra-commentary on Rashi's commentary on the Pentateuch, very similar to Minḥat Yehudah of Yehudah ben Eli῾ezer. Notes Contract of sale with names erased, 1404 (folio 131r). Purchase note (15th-16th century) (folio 139r). Yehudah ha-Rofe᾿ of Aqulia (?): inscribed with his name (folio 2r). Numerous Hebrew inscriptions by Italian hands of the 15th-16th centuries (folios 1*, 2v, 139r).

items.title: Torah (תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentatuech and Hafṭarot, with vowel-points and accents, masorah magna and parva. Mahberet ha-tigan is prefixed. Notes Ezra ben Shalom ben Zekhariah ben Gad ben Meshulam ben Ḥātar Al-Ḥābishi; inscribed in the colophon, folio 305r.

items.title: Mikhlol yofi
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An ethical treatise. Notes Damp entry at lower corner in the first half of the manuscript. Some dark tidemarks. Scribblings, folio 204v.

items.title: Bereshit u-shemot (בראשית ושמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.lang: Hebrew; Jewish Babylonian Aramaic (ca. 200-1200 CE); Judeo-Arabic
items.desc: Genesis and Exodus with Targum Onḳelos and Sa'adyah Ga'on's Arabic translation, with Hebrew grammar Maḥberet al-tigan (מחברת אלתיגאן) at the beginning of the manuscript. Notes Several leaves mutilated, some of them have been mended. Folios 126, 164-165, 167-168, 170 and 177 are later additions on Oriental paper; and several patches of the same paper, same later hand on other folios. Folios 1-3 are a much later addition on modern notebook paper.

items.title: Maḥzor
items.creator: Unknown
items.place: Italy, N. (Reggio di Emilia). Part I: Italy, N. (Reggio di Emilia) Part II: Italy, N. (Bologna) Part III: Italy.
items.desc: A festival prayer book, with miscellaneous works in the margins.

items.title: Torah ʿim perush Rash"i (תורה עם פרוש רש"י)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentateuch with Rashi's commentary. Notes First and last pages are partly stained. Vanilla-dotted hair side of the folios. Doodling and previous owners' notes on folio 231v. Natan Margalit, inscribed in a note, with a date of 18 Tevet 602 (31 December 1841), folio 231v. Antonio Francesco Enriquez, active in 1683-1688 in Urbino, inscribed with a date of 1680 (1687?), folio 231v.

items.title: Torah, hafṭarot u-megilot (תורה, הפטרות ומגילות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Pentateuch with Hafṭarot and the Five Scrolls, with punctuation and accents. Rashi's commentary on the first three chapters of Genesis.

items.title: Nevi᾿im (נביאים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Former and Latter Prophets, accentuated and punctuated. Notes Folio 1r-1v and last few leaves, 346r-349v are not fully preserved.

items.title: Hagadah shel Pesaḥ (הגדה של פסח)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Hagadah for Passover (the 'Ashkenazi Hagadah'). Notes Slightly damaged by damp. Yaʿaḳov Matatyah: mentioned in the colophon (folio 48v).

items.title: Midrash ha-ḥefets (מדרש החפץ)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Midrash ha-ḥefets, a philosophical midrash on the Pentateuch by Zechariah ben Solomon-Rofe, aka Yaḥya ben Saliman. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Gaps after folios 3, 63, 92. Several leaves inlaid with tracing paper for preservation. Folios [i recto], 357r, 357v contain a fragment of Maimonides' Commentary on the Mishnah Midot 2:4-3:1.

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book according to the Italian rite, volume 1.

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book according to the Italian rite, volume 2. Notes Losses to leaves at edges. Usage stains.

items.title: Torah (תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Pentatuech, with vowel-points and accents. Notes The Reverand Theodore Williams of Hendon, (1824): his motto 'Deus alit me' above his armorial shield of a fess, 3 crescents, 3 birds, and a bird on top of the shield (upper cover), and the monogram 'T.W.' and the motto (lower cover). Prince Augustus Frederick (b. 1773, d. 1843), duke of Sussex: his bookplate ([i recto]); his sale, January 18, 1844, lot 365; purchased by Thorpe. Thomas Thorpe: purchased by him for £25 4s: annotated sale catalogue in the History and Classics Department.

items.title: Commentaries on Aristotle.
items.creator: Averroës, 1126-1198
items.dateText: Probably written in Germany, with a colophon dated 1446 (f. 250v); the following pages (f. 251r-269r) may have been written elsewhere and at another time.
items.desc: 15th-century copies of 13th- and 14th-century Hebrew translations of 12th-century commentaries by Averroës on scientific works attributed to Aristotle (De caelo, De mundo, De generatione et corruptione, Meteorologica) and a Hebrew translation of Aristotle's Book 9 of Historia animalium, with frequent marginal notes, some of which show signs of trimming (f. 182r). The end of the volume, with a different scribe and different readers, may have been bound with the rest at some later date; it includes a Hebrew translation of Aristotle's De somno et vigilia, Abraham ibn Ezra's commentary on the Son

items.title: Medical and astronomical miscellany.

items.desc: Compilation of Latin and German texts concerning astronomy, astrology (including resources for the determination of favorable and unfavorable days and a brief treatise on the astrological properties of precious stones attributed in the manuscript to the 8th/9th-century Jewish astrologer Zaël, but also known as the lapidary of Techel), and medicine (including a brief treatise on wine used for medical purposes, attributed in another manuscript to Albertus Magnus). Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger. 1. f.1r-1v: [Circular diagrams of the celestial spheres and the directions] 2. f.2r-7v

Samuel ben ...: inscribed with his name in the colophon as a later correction (folio 429v). items.title: De differentia translationis nostrae ab hebraica littera veteris testamentum
items.creator: Nicholas, of Lyra, approximately 1270-1349
items.desc: This manuscript, made in Germany in the fourteenth century, represents an early copy of works by the Franciscan biblical scholar Nicholas of Lyra (d. 1349). It contains his comparison of the Latin Vulgate and Hebrew texts of the Old Testament (fols. 1r-79v), a tract "against Jews" (fols. 80r-99v), and a commentary on Paul's Epistle to Titus (fols. 100r-120r). It also contains an allegorical concordance of the Old and New Testaments (fols. 121r-182v), perhaps by Remigio de' Girolami or Hugh of Saint Victor. The text itself is simply adorned with red initials, two of which have been embellished

items.title: Ḥeshbon mahalkhot ha-kokhavim
items.creator: Abraham bar Hiyya Savasorda, ca. 1065-ca. 1136
items.dateText: Possibly written in Spain, in the 15th century.
items.desc: 15th-century copy of the second part of a 12th-century two-part treatise, on astronomy (the first part, not present in this manuscript, discussed geography). Includes computations for solar and lunar eclipses between 1135 and 1136. Numerous marginal notes were preserved when the manuscript was trimmed, leaving tabs that have been folded in. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger.

items.title: Marʼeh ha-ofanim ... etc.
items.creator: Sacro Bosco, Joannes de, active 1230
items.dateText: Written in northern Italy in the second quarter of the 15th century (based on watermark information).
items.desc: Hebrew translation of a fundamental treatise on medieval astronomy and cosmology that describes and illustrates the Ptolemaic model of a spherical earth divided into climactic zones at the center of the concentric spheres of the universe. Followed by Ruaḥ ha-ḥen, a 13th-century philosophical work that was a popular introduction to science, here attributed to Yehudah ibn Tibon. It has also been attributed to Jacob ben Abba Mari ben Samson Anatoli and Zeraḥyah ha-Yeṿani. Occasional marginal notes. Final page contains Hebrew notes and pen trials by various hands (f. 22v). Notes Ms. codex. Tit

Kalonymos Finziim (Panziim?; of the Finzi/ Panzi family?: inscribed with his name in an owner's inscription, undated (folio 1r). Laurentius Franguellus (fl. until 1596): inscribed with his name, 1575 (folio 286r); see William Popper, The Censorship of Hebrew Books (New York: KTAV Publishing House, 1969), p. 78, appendix § 105-110. items.title: Megilat Ester ... etc.
items.creator: Unknown
items.dateText: Written in Italy in the 15th century.
items.desc: The Book of Esther written on 3 gatherings removed from a miscellany. The first leaf of the first gathering begins with the last 7 verses of the Book of Lamentations, the work that preceded the Book of Esther in the miscellany. The last leaf of the last gathering has been replaced by an added leaf on which are written 2 piyutim or liturgical poems (Asher heni, whose beginning is not on the leaf, and Shoshanat Yaʻaḳov) used at Purim along with the Book of Esther. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger.

items.title: Reshut le-nishmat le-Fesah leha-R. Binyamin.
items.creator: Biniyamin ben Yoʼav, mi-Roma
items.dateText: Written probably in Italy in the 15th century.
items.desc: Vellum bifolium of 2 non-consecutive leaves from a Siddur according to the Roman rite. Leaf 1 consists of the piyut "Reshut le-nishmat le-Fesah" which is sung after Hallel during Shaḥarit of Pesaḥ ; Leaf 2 verso includes part of the baḳashah "Kol be-ruʼe maʻalah" attributed to Rabbi Shlomo Ibn Gabirol. Notes Ms. leaves. Title from caption. Item cataloged from digital facsimile and existing description. At bottom of leaf 1 verso: "Reshut le-nishmat le-Shavuʻot le-R. Yoʼav min ha-keneset, z.l.h.h."

items.title: Rylands Ashkenazi Haggadah

items.title: Sefer ha-Ḳanon ... etc.
items.creator: Avicenna, 980-1037
items.dateText: Written in Yemen in the 15th century, with additional works and glosses added in the 15th or early 16th century.
items.lang: Predominant work in Hebrew (p. 19-234), with shorter works in Judeo-Arabic
items.desc: Hebrew translation of Avicenna's medical encyclopedia; its division into 4 books is customary for Hebrew translations, but it also has unusual additional sections on drugs, the liver and intestines, and heart diseases. Short medical works in Judeo-Arabic precede Avicenna's work, and a Judeo-Arabic commentary on Galen follows it; these were written by Saʻīd Danūkh, who also wrote marginal notes alongside the Avicenna. Notes Ms. codex. Title for manuscript supplied by cataloger from title for predominant work.

items.title: Sefer ha-Ḳanon ... etc..
items.creator: Avicenna, 980-1037
items.dateText: Written in Italy in the 15th century.
items.desc: Books 1-3 and the beginning of Book 4 of Avicenna's 11th-century comprehensive medical work, translated into Hebrew from Arabic. At the end of the volume is Maimonides's 12th-century treatise on poisons, a much shorter work, which is also lacking the end of the text and was also translated from Hebrew to Arabic, by Mosheh Ibn Tibon. Marginal notes in Hebrew, with at least one in Italian (f. 44v). Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger. 1. f.1r-117v: [Sefer ha-Ḳanon (incomplete) / Avicenna] 2. f.118r-131v: [Maʼamar ha-nikhbad (incomplete) / Moses Maimonides]

items.title: Historia hierosolymitana (History of Jerusalem)
items.creator: Jacques, de Vitry, approximately 1170-1240
items.desc: This manuscript is a humanist copy of the first book of Jacques de Vitry's thirteenth-century history, on Jerusalem or the Orient. The manuscript is written in one hundred chapters whose titles are listed in a table of contents at the beginning of the volume. The colophon at the end of the table of contents (fol. 5r) records that Johannes de Camenago copied the manuscript in 1459 in Milan. At least one leaf of the preface (before fol. 6) is missing, perhaps taken for its decoration. Occasional corrections and notes appear in the margins. Notes Flyleaves are eighteenth-century parchment Flyleav

items.desc: An illuminated specimen of Nahmanides' commentary on the Pentateuch, most likely produced in fifteenth-century Italy. It has been suggested that the elaborate borders containing medallions showing animals, birds and putti were executed by the Florentine miniaturist Francesco d'Antonio del Cherico. Other scholars have suggested another Italian miniaturist, Mariano del Buono. The manuscript was censored by Giovanni Antonio Costanzi in 1769. Notes Marginal corrections have been added by different hands throughout the manuscript. For examples of longer marginal notes see folios 62a, 75a, 83a. Cens

items.title: Quinque libri Egesippi nacione Judei de excidio iudeorum
items.creator: Josephus, Flavius
items.dateText: Written in Italy, possibly Padua, ca. 1460 (based on analysis of illumination by Albinia de la Mare).
items.desc: Digital facsimile of an illuminated manuscript of a Latin translation and adaptation of De bello Judaico with information added from other historians, mostly Latin, traditionally attributed to Egesippus. Contemporary scholarly marginalia and glosses attempt to resolve inconsistencies between this text and the more literal Latin translation of Rufinus. The fourth book of the text, on the application of astronomy to the Jewish calendar, and the fifth book, containing canon tables, are not present in this manuscript. Notes Ms. codex. items.title: Book of Riches
items.creator: Jacob ben Reuben 10..?-11..? caraïte
items.dateText: According to the note on the title page the codex could have been produced in the city of Zolochev in 1539, although this cannot be certain since the title-page was surely a later addition; note on the later title page mentions date and place of creation 299 (=1539)
items.desc: This manuscript contains the Book of Riches (Sefer ha-Osher), the anthology of biblical exegesis compiled by Jacob ben Reuben, a Karaite of the eleventh / twelfth century. The work, which is incomplete at its beginning, is wrongly ascribed to Joseph ben Judah the Karaite on the title page and in the note on folio ia. The title page, written in a different hand and mentioning place and date of copying as Zolochev 1539, was probably added at a later date. One of the anonymous cataloguers of the manuscript read the placename mentioned there (זולצעוו) as Gozlow (Eupatoria). Notes Different type of

items.title: Sidur (סדור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A service book according to the Yemenite rite. Notes Most of leaves partly damaged (lower margin). Stained. Se῾adyah ben Yehudah Al-Ladani: inscribed in the colophon, folio 248r. Two records of birth, dated 1828 (1517) and 1833 (1522) respectively, in the main scribe's handwriting, folio 248r (below the colophon). A record of transfer of the manuscript between the owners, Se῾adyah ben Yehudah Al-Ladani and Se῾adyah ben Mantsur Al-Ḥawidi, dated 1544, folio 248r. Folio 1 and 249 contain portions of Leviticus XIV and XV, Hebrew text with Targum to each verse, both leaves are mutilated. Folio 2 (v

items.title: Collection of philosophical writings.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Philosophical writings. Notes Tidemarks at head and often on an entire leaf, without loss of text. Stains on board cloth.

items.title: Perush ha-Torah ῾al derekh ha-ḳabalah (פרוש התורה על דרך הקבלה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A kabbalistic commentary on the Pentateuch, consisting of excerpts from the Zohar, Tiḳune Zohar, Sefer Temunah, and other works. Notes Stained. Fra Gir. Durazzano, active in 1640-41 in Modena-Reggio: inscribed with his name, 1640? (folio 214r); see 89. Censored by Renato da Modena: inscribed with his name, 'Visto per mi fr. Renato da Mod., 1626' (folio 214r); see 132-133. Luigi da Bologna: inscribed with his name: 'Visto per mi fra Luigi del 1600' (folio 214v); a converted Jew working for the Inquisition as censor (active 1590-1609), see 104, and appendix § 112-117.

items.title: Meḳitse nirdamim
items.creator: Alguadez, Meir ben Solomon, fl. 1390-1410

items.desc: Collection of prescriptions from the 14th-century physician Meir ben Solomon Alguadez and one of his students, originally written in Spanish, in the Hebrew translation of Joseph ha-Kohen completed in 1546, also with Latin translations. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger.

items.title: Tsurat ha-arets ... etc.
items.creator: Abraham bar Hiyya Savasorda, ca. 1065-ca. 1136
items.dateText: Possibly written in Gratz, in the second half of the 16th century, after the first edition of Erasmus Reinhold's Theoricae novae planetarum Georgii Purbacchii Germani in 1542, some of which is copied alongside the Hebrew translation of Peurbach (f. 188r-196v), on paper with watermarks similar to those on paper produced in Gratz in the 1580s and 1590s (Les Enluminures).
items.desc: Collection of astronomical texts in Hebrew from source texts ranging from the 11th to the 16th centuries. Subjects include geography, the Ptolemaic model of the universe, solar and lunar eclipses, and the astrolabe. Frequent marginal notes and glosses, some from copied from known commentaries to these works. Notes Ms. codex. Title for manuscript from title supplied for first work by cataloger. Watermark Similar to Briquet, Aigle à deux têtes 285 (f. 45, 48; Gratz, 1594-1598; Millstatt (Carinthie), 1597-1600; Vienna, 1599) and Aigle à deux têtes 282 (Gratz, 1580; Osnabruck, 1588); similar t

items.title: Bet Elohim
items.creator: Almosnino, Moses ben Baruch, ca. 1515-ca. 1580
items.desc: Commentary on Solomon ben Abraham Avigdor's Hebrew translation of Joannes de Sacro Bosco's Sphaera mundi, followed by a commentary on a Hebrew translation, possibly by Almosnino himself, of Georg von Peurbach's Theoricae novae planetarum, with the assistance of Aharon Afia. This copy was made 5 years after the works were composed in 1546 (f. 168r). Almosnino's commentary on Sacro Bosco includes the earliest reference in Jewish literature to Amerigo Vespucci and America (f. 23v), among discussion of other geographical discoveries. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger.

items.title: Minhagim (מנהגים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Minhagim relating to the ritual for the fasts and festivals as used in Germany.

items.title: Sefer ha-Peli᾿ah.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Sefer ha-Peli᾿ah. Notes Stained. Avraham ibn Nun, Yitsḥaḳ Motsri: inscribed in a note, folio 262r. Leṿi ben Avraham ben Ele῾azar ha-Leṿi: inscribed, folio 1v.

items.title: Midrash ha-gadol (מדרש הגדול)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: First part of Midrash ha-gadol on Exodus. Notes Imperfect at the end. Folio 24r contains a narrative on Moses, probably inserted here by the scribe (not in other versions of the same treatise). Two leaves, folio 156r-157v, are appended, containing fragments on the computation of various eras by Muhamad ibn Abi Bakr al-Farisi, written ca. 16th century. The fly-leaf at the end, folio fse001r, contains a small part of the commentary of Rashi on the last chapter of Deuteronomy. Afizi ben Shalom ben Yosef ben Zekharyah, writing for Se῾adyah ben Shalom, Daṿid ben Shalom and Yosef ben Shalom, inscrib

items.title: Pesiḳta rabata.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Collection of Midrash with an introduction in Venetian dialect (Italian-Spanish mix). Although identified at the top of the first page as Pesikta de-rav Kahanah, an inscription at the end of the text refers to it as Pesikta rabata. An introduction to the work is found on leaves 186-195. Notes Ms. codex. Title from colophon. Although identified at the top of the first page as Pesiḳta de-rav Ḳahanah, an inscription at the end of the text refers to it as Pesiḳta rabata. An introduction to the work is found on leaves 186-195. Byname: London manuscript. Item cataloged from digital facsimile and

items.title: פרוש ויקרא (Perush va-yiḳra)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A fragment of Judeo-Arabic commentary of Japheth ben Ali ha-Levi on Leviticus (5:15-15:33, and 25). Notes Lacunae after folios: 10, 29, 37, 57, 92, 124 and 147.

items.title: Targum ῾aravi la-Torah ve-ḥamesh megilot
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An Judeo-Arabic translation of the Torah and the Five Scrolls. Notes Damp, with tidemarks across the leaves. Usage stains, mould spotting. Slight worming. Losses at edges, all infilled before rebinding.

items.title: An Arabic translation of the Former and Latter Prophets, with commentaries.
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: An Arabic translation of the Former and Latter Prophets, with commentaries. Notes Damp at head and gutter. Losses to damp and mould at ends, repaired and infilled.

items.title: Compilation of texts on logic and astrology
items.creator: Caspi, Joseph, ca. 1280-ca. 1340
items.desc: Codex in Sephardi script containing a compilation of texts on logic and astronomy, written and partly authored by Samuel ben Sa'adiah ibn Danan in Fez in 1576. Notes Marginal notes 24b-25b, 26b, 27b-28a, 29b-31a, 33a-b, 34b-36b, 47a, 49a, 75b, 95b in different hands. Occasional interlinear notes. Paper with visible chainlines and laid lines. Watermark Grapes watermark on f.89.

items.title: Sefer Mitsṿot gadol (בפר מצות גדול)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Sefer Mitsṿot gadol, also called Gan ῾eden, of Aaron ben Elijah, the Karaite. Notes A French description of the manuscript, pasted on folio 1v. Eliyah Yerushalmi: inscribed, folio 2v. Folio 69v: a record of sale of the manuscript, between Mosheh Rabitsi ben Ye᾿uda Nur and Avraham Yerushalmi. Folio 265r: a record of sale of the manuscript between Eliyah Tishbi and Hillel ben Mosheh Hillel.

items.title: Sefer ha-Peli᾿ah (ספר הפליאה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Sefer ha-Peli᾿ah. Notes Stained. Slightly water-damaged. Ya῾aḳov ben Mordekhai Pogeṭṭi: inscribed in the colophon, folio 310v. Censored by Boniforte del Asinary: Italian censor (active 1575-1599 in Asti): inscribed with his name: 'Visto et coretto per me Boniforte del Asinari: inscribed (folio 310v). Censored by Hieronimus Carolus: inscribed with his name: 'Fr. Hier. Carolus Asti Die 19 Febr. 1582': inscribed (folio 310v).

items.title: Ketuvim (כתובים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Hagiographa, provided with vowel-points and accents, masorah magna and parva. Notes Several larger lacunae. Some leaves are imperfectly preserved. Folio 1r: a doodle page. Folio 1r, a note of ownership: לקדש כניסת יחיא אלטירי ן' אברהם מן אלנדרים אלדרי בנצר יוסף ן' סאלם אלכרנבא יצ"ו Similar inscription is found on folio 97v.

items.title: Nevi᾿im (נביאים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Former Prophets: Hebrew text with ordinary punctuation and Targum with simple superlinear punctuation in alternate verses; with masorah magna and parva. Maḥberet ha-tigan is prefixed. Notes Folios 249-254 are not fully preserved. Se'adyah ben David ben Zekharyah ben Amram al-Akhlufi; inscribed with his name in the colophon, folio 254r.

items.title: Nevi᾿im (נביאים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Former Prophets with Targum, punctuated, with masorah magna and parva. Notes Folios 1-3, 209-215 are imperfectly preserved. Folios 217r-217v contain doodles. Comments of a non-masoretic character are found throughout, e.g. 42v, 45r. A Hebrew subscription on folio 216v, with a date of 18 Marheshvan 1954, i.e. 1642, and the name of the city of Ma'abar, due to a later hand. Aharon ha-Kohen, inscribed with his name on folio 1r.

items.title: Yiddish translation of De humani corporis fabrica librorum epitome
items.creator: Vesalius, Andreas, 1514-1564
items.dateText: Written in Germany, perhaps in the Hesse region between 1590 and 1595 (based on watermark, Les Enluminures).
items.lang: Yiddish, in Ashkenazi Hebrew characters, with a few words in Latin (f. 2r)
items.desc: Treatise on anatomy intended for medical students, abridged by Vesalius from his own larger work. This Yiddish translation is probably based on a pirated German translation published in Nuremberg in 1551 under the title Anatomia deudsch. Organized in the same 6 chapters as the original Latin epitome of Vesalius and the Anatomia deudsch, concerning bones and cartilage, ligaments and muscles, the digestive system, the heart, the brain, and the reproductive system. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger. Watermark Similar to Briquet Aigle 154-155 (Babenhausen, 1593; Eltsville, near Mainz, 1

items.title: Keter Torah (כתר תורה)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A commentary on the Pentateuch. Notes Last 20 pages partially stained. Folio 1r - a doodle-page. Folio [ii recto] (modern paper flyleaf) contains a Hebrew description of the contents of this volume. A German description of the manuscript from Moritz Steinschneider's catalogue is glued to folio [ii verso]. A note of sale, recording Eliyahu ben Shelomoh and Ḳerapula (?) bat Benyamin, folio 1r.

items.title: Edict concerning Jews in Livorno and Pisa
items.creator: Ferdinando I, Grand-Duke of Tuscany, 1549-1609
items.dateText: Written in Italy, possibly in Florence, after the edict was issued in 1593.
items.desc: Copy of an edict inviting foreign merchants, especially Jews, to settle in Livorno and Pisa, and defining their rights and privileges. The original edict was issued from the ducal palace in Florence, dated 10 June 1593, and consists of 44 clauses. The rights and privileges included amnesty for offenses committed previously, freedom from debts incurred elsewhere, free safe conduct in Livorno, the right to conduct business throughout Tuscany, the same rights and privileges in conducting foreign trade as others in Tuscany, protection from extraordinary levies beyond the usual taxes, exemption fro

items.title: Edicts concerning Jews in Mantua
items.creator: Vincenzo I Gonzaga, Duke of Mantua, 1562-1612
items.desc: Official copy of an edict issued by Vincenzo Gonzaga, duke of Mantua, in 1594 renewing privileges and amnesties of the Jews of Mantua, who were allowed to work as bankers, merchants, and butchers. The contemporary edict is preceded by copies of 2 earlier renewals, the first issued by Guglielmo Gonzaga, Vincenzo's father and the previous duke, in Goito in 1587, and the second issued by Vincenzo in 1590. All 3 copies, perhaps written by a chancery scribe, are signed by ducal chancellor Matthaeus Gentilis. Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger. Watermark Crown with countermark of IBC with

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown

items.title: Maḥzor (מחזור)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A festival prayer book, according to the rite of Algiers. Notes Foxed paper. Yosef Neḳaṿah (?): inscribed, folios 56r, 188r. An anonymous entry, dated 8 Adar 5680 (1708): inscribed, folio 188r.

items.title: Megilat Ester (מגלת אסתר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Book of Esther. Notes Small diagonal creases at edges. One usage stain. Ivory with lacunae in bone, and also small splits where dried out.

items.title: Midrash (מדרש)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Midrashim in Hebrew and Arabic on Leviticus, Numbers and Deuteronomy, followed by a short midrashic commentary on the Hafṭarot. Notes Imperfect at the end. Slightly water-damaged. Se'adyah ben Shelomoh: probably a compiler of this manuscript: inscribed, folio 61r. The Judeo-Arabic sentence at the end of Deuteronomy, folio 189v, implies that the author had employed a copyist.

items.title: Midrash Devarim (מדרש דברים)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Parts of David ben Abraham Maimuni's homiletico-agadic commentary on Deuteronomy. Notes Imperfect at the beginning. Folios missing after folio 304. Slightly water-damaged. Last leaves slightly worm-eaten. The last six leaves belonged to a different manuscript. Contain midrashic expositions on parashah Ṿa-yelekh and Ha᾿azinu. Mikha᾿el ben Mosheh: inscribed, folio 2r. Raḥamim ben Shemu᾿el Malkiowner: inscribed, folio 369r.

items.title: Midrash Shemot (מדרש שמות)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Parts of David ben Abraham Maimuni's homiletico-agadic commentary on Exodus. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Lacunae after folios 93, 102, 150. Slightly damaged.

items.title: Midrash ha-ḥefets (מדרש החפץ)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Midrash ha-ḥefets, a philosophical midrash on the Pentateuch by Zechariah ben Solomon-Rofe, aka Yaḥya ben Saliman. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. Several folios more or less damaged.

items.title: Midrash Ṿa-yiḳra (מדרש ויקרא)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Parts of David ben Abraham Maimuni's homiletico-agadic commentary on Leviticus. Notes Imperfect at the beginning and at the end. First and last leaf damaged.

items.title: Milon ῾ivri-latini (מלון עברי-לטיני)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: A Hebrew-Latin dictionary. Notes Darkening from damp. Dirt entry at head.

items.title: Mishle Sendabar (משלי סנדבר)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Ethical writings. Notes Corroding ink.

items.title: Mishpeṭe Shemuʾel (משפטי שמואל)
items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: Mishpeṭe Shemuʾel, containing textual notes, explanations and diagrams on some tractates of Seder Zeraʿim, by Shemuʾel ben Avraham ha-Kohen of Fez. Notes Slightly stained. Shabtai Elḥanan Sangineti: inscribed, folio 1r. Folio 75r: two notes, in cursive writing, recommending the work for printing, the first signed by Aharon Ḳazes, and the second by Ya῾aḳov Tsarfati.

Notes Folios missing after folio 161, 173, 222. Slightly stained. [...] ben Yeshu῾ah Firūz: inscribed, folio 2r. Shelomoh Kohen ben Yosef Kohen ben Mordekhai Kohen: inscribed, folio 1v.

items.creator: Unknown
items.desc: The Muḳadimat, or introductions (in Arabic and Hebrew), to the weekly parashiyot of the Pentateuch, by Shemu᾿el ha-Rofe al-Maghribi in three uniform volumes (volume 2). Notes Folios missing after folio 236. Slightly stained. Avraham ha-Kohen wrote the present manuscript for his son, Yosef. The name of the latter is inscribed under the title of each Muḳadimah. [...] ben Yeshu῾ah Firūz: inscribed, folio 2r of volume 1. Shelomoh Kohen ben Yosef Kohen ben Mordekhai Kohen: inscribed, folio 1v of volume 1.

Shelomoh Kohen ben Yosef Kohen ben Mordekhai Kohen: inscribed, folio 1v of volume 1.

items.desc: Anonymous polemic against the treatment of christaos descendentes do sangue hebreu (New Christians of Hebrew blood, converts from Judaism to Catholicism and their descendants) by the Portuguese Inquisition. Notes Ms. codex. Title from caption title (f. 1r). Cassuto identified this work with the Portuguese section of Noticias reconditas y posthumas published in London in 1722 and sometimes attributed to the Portuguese Jesuit Antonio Vieira (note by Cassuto, who gives the date as 1720, inside upper cover), who pleaded with Pope Clement X on behalf of the New Christians. items.creator: Moldovan Family Judaica Collection (University of Pennsylvania)
items.dateText: Written in Italy during the mid to late 17th century, perhaps part of a larger compendium ("ḳovets") of kabbalistic manuscript writing.
items.lang: Hebrew, with some words of Aramaic
items.desc: This is a manuscript of instruction, divine and kabbalistic names and other meditations (kaṿanot) for the Passover Seder. The manuscript begins with an introduction ("Berikh shemeh", f. 1r-2r), and is followed by instructions with headings used from the Passover Haggadah; begining the Kiddush order with a specially created Aramaic poem after the Atḳinu seʻudata stanza (f. 2r-3v), followed by a long supplication (f. 3v-4v); following this is the scheme of the Seder with instructions and verses ("Ḳadesh urḥats karpas yaḥats" etc.). The Seder ends with the abbreviation a.k.y.r. (amen ken ye-

items.title: Kiryat Sefer

items.desc: This is a halakhic manuscript guiding scribes through the technicalities of writing a copy of the Torah scroll by hand. The first item is Kiryat Sefer by Menahem ben Shelomoh Meiri. It is incomplete, running up to and including ma'amar 4, helek 3 (folios 1a-41a). Curiously, while ma'amar 5 is missing, there follow two parts which might as well have been written by Meiri, since they form an organic whole with the preceding text. items.desc: Seventeenth-century Sephardi illuminated manuscript. It comprises various Hebrew texts in the fields of polemical literature, philosophy, secular and liturgical poetry, travel literature and mathematics. A different scribe added at the beginning of the manuscript a short anonymous Hebrew essay refuting the idea of eternal punishment as well as a joint letter by Shema'yah di Medina and Azariah Figo sent from Venice to Isaac Aboab in Amsterdam urging the latter to give up his views on the same issue. The treatises usually start after several folios have been left blank. The manuscript is closely

European forms of arabic numerals appear in the text on one page (f. 36r). A former wrapper or divider, now bound in at the beginning (f. 1, 40), has a title in a later hand that suggests that this manuscript is the second part of a larger work, seconded by an Italian note in pencil, Discorso 2o (f. 1r). Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by Yacov Fuchs (National Library of Israel). Watermark Crown over 3 fleurs-de-lis in a heart shape, all surrounded by crossed le

items.title: Pesukim ṿe-tserufe shemot ʻal derekh ha-ḳabalah le-Rosh ha-Shanah ule-Yamim Noraʼim.
items.creator: Yaḥya, Avraham, 17th century
items.dateText: Written likely in northern Italy in the early 17th century by Abraham Yaḥya, or Yaḥiya; perhaps he was a relative of the prominent (and widespread) Yaḥiya family, with roots in 12th century Lisbon whose descendants at this period were prominent in Portugal, Spain, Italy, and Turkey. The dating of the manuscript is based on Yaḥya's friendship with a R. Natan Spira (f. 7r); likely a reference to R. Natan Note Spira of Kraków, a famed kabbalist and author of Megaleh ʻamuḳot, who lived 1585-1633. Also, the poem Yedid nefesh is attributed in this manuscript (f. 3r) to R. Eleazar Azikri (1533-1600), who printed it for the first time in 1600-1 in a volume he authored (Sefer ḥaredim, Venice: Daniel Zanetti, 1600-1601).
items.desc: This is a manuscript of a liturgical miscellany with many diverse kabbalistic elements to it. It begins with a silent prayer for the cantor on the High Holidays, supplying verses to meditate on and guaranteeing that the audience will not have a harmful verdict ("gezerah raʻah") for the upcoming year (f. 1r-1v). The prayer ends with additional texts, titled "Ḳabalot", of verses to recite with supplications; the first attributed to R.T. items.title: Sefer ha-Peli'ah Book of Wonder

items.desc: Seventeenth-century Hebrew codex containing the Kabbalistic text Sefer ha-Peli'ah ("Book of Wonder"). Folios Flyleaf 1a-Flyleaf 2b contain references to the Sefer ha-Kanah. Many Kabbalists used Sefer ha-Kanah to refer to both the Sefer ha-Peli'ah and the Sefer ha-Kanah. The texts were apparently written by the same anonymous Kabbalist toward the end of the fourteenth century or the beginning of the fifteenth. items.creator: Mendoza, Francisco de, d. 1566
items.desc: A French translation of the Spanish work El tizón de la nobleza española, o, Máculas y sambenitos de sus linajes composed in the mid-16th century. This manuscript is a genealogical investigation into the origins of the most prominent aristocratic families of Spain and Portugal, presented by the author to king Philip II. This is a controversial treatise because, in seeking to defend the purity of the lineage of his own nephew (maquis of Cañette), Mendoza traces the origins of the Iberian aristocracy to Jewish and Moorish ancestors. items.desc: This is a manuscript of collected writings and correspondence of Solomon Joseph Ḳarpi (then spelled Carpi) of Livorno. The opening letter is addressed to his brother, Yiśraʼel Ḥayim Ḳarpi, called "the great emissary" in Aramaic ("hai sheluḥah rabah," f. 1r) on the title page which includes a note about the manuscript's creation. Writing about Shabtai Tsevi (Izmir 1626 - Ulcinj 1676, f. v-22r), he records testimonies about a trip through Istanbul (f. 5v-6v) and its surrounding villages (f. 7r-7v) and Shabtai in Italy, including Casale Monferrato (f. 8r-16v). items.desc: This is a manuscript of prayers to be recited on the evening of Hoshana Rabba, which is the seventh day of Sukkot. The manuscript is a selection of Psalms, out of order, complimenting the themes of the day and the piyutim. the theme of the day is often penitential and therefore some of the liturgy contained in the manuscript is penitential (for example, the piyut "Raḥamana idkar lan," f. 4v). Notes Ms. codex. Title supplied by cataloger.

items.title: Seliḥot.
